LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CAIFORNIA GAMBLING CONTROL COMMISISON'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS OVERRULED EXCEPT AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, AS TO WHICH IT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS UNOPPOSED. [SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.] HOWEVER, BECAUSE THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE RAISES ISSUES IDENTICAL TO THOSE RAISED IN THE PENDING APPEAL, THE COURT ON ITS OWN MOTION STAYS ALL FURTHER PROCEEDINGS ON THIS ACTION PENDING FINAL DISPOSITION OF THE APPEAL. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Maureen Harrington, Esq. (408-995-5600; MHarrington@greenfieldlaw.com) of Greenfield LLP for Petitioner, Garden City, Inc. dba Casino M8trix.
James Waian, Esq. (619-738-9335; James.Waian@doj.ca.gov) Deputy Attorney General for Respondent, California Gambling Control Commission.
Christine Peek, Esq. (cpeek@mcmanislaw.com) for Real Party in Interest, Sutter's Place, Inc. dba Bay 101.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CAIFORNIA GAMBLING CONTROL COMMISISON'S DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION:

Respondent California Gambling Control Commission's demurrer to Petitioner's writ of mandate is overruled except as to the cause of action for declaratory relief, as to which it is sustained without leave to amend as unopposed. Respondent's res judicata and collateral estoppel claims fail because the prior action, in which Petitioner was named as a real party in interest and in which it participated in briefing and argument, is pending on appeal. (Sutter's Place, Inc. v. California Gambling Control Commission (S.F. Super. Ct. No. CPF-21-517433, Court of Appeal No. A163287.) It is settled California law that a pending appeal is not a final judgment for purposes of res judicata and collateral estoppel. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1049 [an action is deemed to be pending from the time of its commencement until its final determination on appeal]; Franklin & Franklin v. 7-Eleven Owners for Fair Franchising (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 1168, 1174 ["in California the rule is that the finality required to invoke the preclusive bar of res judicata is not achieved until an appeal from the trial court judgment has been exhausted or the time to appeal has expired"] [internal quotations and citations omitted]; Swaffield v. Universal Ecsco Corp. (1969) 271 Cal.App.2d 147, 159-160 ["the California rule is that a judgment is not final for purposes of collateral estoppel until final disposition on appeal."].)

However, because the petition for writ of mandate raises issues identical to those raised in the pending appeal, the Court on its own motion stays all further proceedings on this action pending final disposition of the appeal. (See Freiberg v. City of Mission Viejo (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1489 ["Trial courts generally have the inherent power to stay proceedings in the interests of justice and to promote judicial efficiency."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
